semanticCore: Hanging clusters of dates, bunch of fruit, possessing property, or gaining wealth

keyDerivations.meaning: Hanging cluster of dates, bunch of dates from a palm branch

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمِنَ النَّخْلِ مِنْ طَلْعِهَا قِنْوَانٌ دَانِيَةٌ (9:99): And from the palm trees - of its spathe - clusters of dates hanging low/accessible.
summaryEn: The root refers to the heavy clusters of dates (Qinwan), cited as an accessible botanical blessing that hangs within easy reach for harvest.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى عذوق النخلة المتدلية المحملة بالرطب والتمر السهل جنيها (قنو)، معدداً ثمار النخل الدانية كنعمة غذائية وجمالية متجددة.
rawText: أَقْنَى in the prov., خَلَاؤُكَ أَقْنَى لِحَيائِكَ i. q. أَلْزَمُ [as meaning Most preservative: see that prov. in art. خلو, and see قَنِىَ الحَيَآءَ, above]. (S in art. خلو, and Meyd.) إِسْتَقْنَوَ 10 see 1. اقْتَنَى 8 He gained, acquired, or got, for himself, (S, K,) or took for himself, (Mgh,) property, or camels, &c., (S, Mgh,) as a permanent stock, for propagation, (Mgh,) not for merchandise: (S, Mgh:) he made it to be in his possession, not to depart from his hand: (TA:) he acquired it for himself permanently, or for a permanence. See 1. فِنْوَةٌ : see قِنِيَّةٌ. قَانَى 3 : see قَانَأَ. قَنًا of the nose: see شَمَمٌ. === قنا وُشَّق a name given in Egypt to وُشَّق; also called أُشَّق and أُشَّج. (TA in art. اشق.) قَنَاةٌ A spear-shaft; (Mgh;) a spear (T, S. K) that is hollow, like a cane; (Az, in TA;) a spear with a head affixed to it. (Msb.) === Hence, A subterranean channel, or conduit, for water. (Mgh.) === [And A pipe.] === قَنَاةُ الكُوزِ The بُلْبُل [or spout] of the كوز [or mug], that pours forth the water. (M, K, in art. بل.) === قَنَاةٌ, said to signify بَقَرَةٌ وَحْشِيَّةٌ: see فَنَاةٌ. قَنِىَ الحَيَآءَ 1 He kept to the sense of shame, or modesty; (S, K;) he preserved it: and i. q. اِسْتَحْيَى: and 🡻اِسْتَقْنَى he kept to [or preserved] his sense of shame, or modesty. (TA.) === قَنَا غَنَمًا, and 🡻اِقْتَنَى, He took for himself, got, or acquired, sheep, or goats [for a permanent possession], not for sale. (JK.) === قَنَا, aor. يَقْنُو, inf. n. قُنُوٌّ; a dial. var. of قَنَأَ, q. v. (TA.) قِنْيَةٌ and 🡻قِنْوَةٌ Sheep, or goats, taken for oneself, gotten, or acquired, [for a permanent possession,] not for sale. (JK.) مُقَانَاةٌ The weaving with one thread white and one thread black. (T, voce نِيرٌ.)
